page.title: Marbella Hen Party Yacht Catering: Menus, Costs, and Tips

data.meta_description: Plan your Marbella hen party yacht catering with menus, costs, and tips. From €749/2h with drinks and snacks included. Book your boat today.
data.summary: A practical guide to catering for a hen party on a private yacht in Marbella, covering menu options, costs, and tips from local experts. All charters include skipper, drinks, snacks, and VAT.
key_facts: All charters include water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks.
Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) from €749/2h or €2,299/8h, all inclusive.
Tier B boats (Mangusta 80 variants) from €4,719/4h, includes a free jet ski.
Upgrade to premium catering (gourmet platters, sushi, paella) on request via WhatsApp.
Departures from Puerto Banús, with stops at Cala del Faro, Cala Cortés, or Cabopino.
Maximum capacity ranges from 5 to 12 guests depending on the boat.
No corkage fees for bringing your own drinks, but check with the team first.
All prices include 21% Spanish IVA – no hidden taxes.

faq.q: What catering is included on a Marbella hen party yacht charter?
faq.a: Every charter includes water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks such as crisps, nuts, and olives. You can upgrade to a full catering package with gourmet platters, sushi, or paella. Contact us via WhatsApp to arrange a custom menu.

faq.q: Can I bring my own food and drinks on board?
faq.a: Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food and additional drinks. There is no corkage fee, but we recommend checking with the team first to ensure compatibility with the boat’s facilities. The included drinks and snacks are already provided.

faq.q: How much does catering cost on a hen party yacht in Marbella?
faq.a: Basic catering is included in the charter price. Upgraded menus, such as a seafood platter or a paella lunch, are priced on request. For example, a gourmet platter for 10 people typically costs €150–€300. Contact us for a quote.

faq.q: What types of food work best for a hen party on a yacht?
faq.a: Light, easy-to-eat options work best: finger food, sushi, tapas, fresh fruit, and charcuterie boards. Avoid heavy, messy dishes. Many groups also enjoy a paella cooked on board or a barbecue at anchor. We can arrange any of these.

faq.q: Can we have a cake or decorations on the yacht?
faq.a: Yes, you can bring a cake and decorations. Let us know in advance so we can ensure there is space and a fridge if needed. Balloons and banners are fine, but please avoid confetti or glitter that is hard to clean.

faq.q: Is there a minimum spend for catering upgrades?
faq.a: No, there is no minimum spend for catering upgrades. You can order as little or as much as you like. Prices start from around €15 per person for a basic upgrade. Ask us for a tailored quote.

faq.q: What drinks are included, and can we bring our own alcohol?
faq.a: Included drinks are water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, and cava. You can bring your own spirits, cocktails, or champagne. The boat has a cooler and ice. No corkage fee applies.

faq.q: Do you cater for dietary requirements or allergies?
faq.a: Yes, we can accommodate vegetarian, vegan, gluten-free, and other dietary needs. Please inform us at least 48 hours before the charter. The team will work with local caterers to ensure everyone is catered for.

data.body_html:
Planning a hen party in Marbella and wondering about the food and drinks on board? You are in the right place. Every yacht charter from our fleet includes a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, and a selection of drinks and snacks – all covered in the price. Whether you want a simple afternoon with cava and olives or a full paella feast, we explain the options, costs, and tips so you can focus on celebrating. All prices include 21% Spanish IVA.

What catering is included in every charter?

Every booking comes with water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks (crisps, nuts, olives). This is included in the rates – no extra charges. For example, a Tier A boat like the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 guests) costs €749 for 2 hours or €2,299 for 8 hours, all inclusive. You can bring your own additional drinks or food if you wish, with no corkage fee.

Upgraded catering options for hen parties

If you want something more special, we can arrange upgraded catering. Popular choices include:

  • Gourmet platters (charcuterie, cheese, seafood) – from €150 for 10 people
  • Sushi platters – from €20 per person
  • Paella cooked on board – from €25 per person, minimum 8 guests
  • Barbecue at anchor – from €30 per person
  • Fresh fruit platters and desserts – from €10 per person

All upgrades are priced on request. Contact us via WhatsApp to discuss your menu and get a quote.

Which boats suit a hen party best?

For a group of up to 12, the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 guests) is a popular choice. It includes a free jet ski and costs €4,719 for a minimum 4-hour charter. For smaller groups, the Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 guests) from €749/2h is ideal. All boats depart from Puerto Banús, with stops at Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés for swimming.

Bringing your own cake, decorations, and drinks

Yes, you can bring a cake – just let us know so we can prepare fridge space. Decorations like balloons and banners are fine, but please avoid confetti or glitter. You can also bring your own spirits or champagne. The boat has a cooler and ice. No corkage fee applies.

Dietary requirements and allergies

We cater for vegetarian, vegan, gluten-free, and other dietary needs. Inform us at least 48 hours before the charter. The team works with local caterers to ensure everyone is covered. For example, a gluten-free platter or vegan tapas can be arranged.

How to book and customise your catering

To book, choose your boat from our Marbella yacht charter fleet. After booking, you will receive a WhatsApp link to discuss catering upgrades. Payment is by bank transfer or card. All prices include 21% IVA. For any questions, contact us directly.

Frequently asked questions

What catering is included on a Marbella hen party yacht charter?

Every charter includes water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks such as crisps, nuts, and olives. You can upgrade to a full catering package with gourmet platters, sushi, or paella. Contact us via WhatsApp to arrange a custom menu.

Can I bring my own food and drinks on board?

Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food and additional drinks. There is no corkage fee, but we recommend checking with the team first to ensure compatibility with the boat’s facilities. The included drinks and snacks are already provided.

How much does catering cost on a hen party yacht in Marbella?

Basic catering is included in the charter price. Upgraded menus, such as a seafood platter or a paella lunch, are priced on request. For example, a gourmet platter for 10 people typically costs €150–€300. Contact us for a quote.

What types of food work best for a hen party on a yacht?

Light, easy-to-eat options work best: finger food, sushi, tapas, fresh fruit, and charcuterie boards. Avoid heavy, messy dishes. Many groups also enjoy a paella cooked on board or a barbecue at anchor. We can arrange any of these.

Can we have a cake or decorations on the yacht?

Yes, you can bring a cake and decorations. Let us know in advance so we can ensure there is space and a fridge if needed. Balloons and banners are fine, but please avoid confetti or glitter that is hard to clean.

Is there a minimum spend for catering upgrades?

No, there is no minimum spend for catering upgrades. You can order as little or as much as you like. Prices start from around €15 per person for a basic upgrade. Ask us for a tailored quote.

What drinks are included, and can we bring our own alcohol?

Included drinks are water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, and cava. You can bring your own spirits, cocktails, or champagne. The boat has a cooler and ice. No corkage fee applies.

Do you cater for dietary requirements or allergies?

Yes, we can accommodate vegetarian, vegan, gluten-free, and other dietary needs. Please inform us at least 48 hours before the charter. The team will work with local caterers to ensure everyone is catered for.
